student_name: Eliane Portela Nogueira Lima de Brito
hub_subtitle: Business English for a doctor rebuilding her professional voice

lesson.menu_title: The Clinic Is Happening — Plans, Timelines & Business Vision
lesson.menu_desc: The four future forms (going to, present continuous, will, plan/intend/expect to) and pitching your vision -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 3 -- The Clinic Is Happening
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 3 -- The Clinic Is Happening

listenings.text: Hi, I'm Daniel, and I founded a medical-device company three years ago. Let me walk you through our launch timeline. Right now, we are finishing the design of our new lab equipment. Next quarter, we are going to start production, and we are opening our first facility in Austin in the fall. Our seed funding is already in place, so we plan to be fully operational by early next year. I won't pretend it is easy, but the market is growing fast, and I believe demand will double within five years. My advice? Set clear milestones, protect your timeline, and keep every stakeholder informed. If you stay on track, the hard work pays off.

Hi Eliane, this is Rachel from the equipment team in Boston. I am calling about the timeline for your new facility. Good news: your state-of-the-art incubators are ready, and we are shipping them next week. Installation is going to take about ten days, so you will be operational sooner than we expected. I am also sending a quote for the second phase of your rollout. One of our main priorities is keeping you on track, so please call me if anything changes. We are really excited to be part of this venture. Talk soon, and congratulations on the progress.
